# Extracao: transcription_mp4_39__Trait_38_-_Great_Salespeople_value_the_connections_in_their_community_as_their_main_aim_1_of_com

- **Fonte:** transcription_mp4_39__Trait_38_-_Great_Salespeople_value_the_connections_in_their_community_as_their_main_aim_1_of_com.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT
- **Tamanho:** 0 MB

---

Great salespeople value connections within the community. You know, the community, the neighborhood they work in, the city they live in, maybe the industry or just their office. They look at that community, where they sell, where they work as their main aim of commerce. This is where their revenue comes from. People, people, people. The greats are all about people. They're about connections and relationships. They know about this obscurity problem. They know obscurity is the demon. They know how to get attention. They connect it. They know how to make, develop, maintain and protect relationships. The greats are unbelievable at this and something I'm still working on today. 25 years, 30 years I've been in business now and I'm just getting to the point where I'm like, oh my gosh, I got to pay attention to these relationships. Don't make the mistake I have. Be great now at building those relationships and then combining all these other attributes, then insisting that that relationship becomes your customer.
